REPRODUCED at b0589665: tile handle computed style 6x6px, radius 100pc, 1px white border, opacity 1 - visible circle on the tile edge. Cause: the code-split lets xyflow's base stylesheet inject AFTER styles.css, so the same-specificity handle flatten-override loses the cascade race it used to win by load order.

FIXED: one order-proof 2-class rule (.react-flow .react-flow__handle) — opacity 0, no border, pointer-events none, 5px anchor box kept. Computed style now opacity 0 / 0px border; 7 edges still drawn; the ▸N tabs intact. Outranks xyflow's base stylesheet regardless of which order the lazy graph chunk injects it.

B/pass on final fix commit 0a7e5c15: 16 React Flow handles remain as edge anchors only; computed pointer-events=none, opacity=0, borderWidth=0px, transparent background; elementsFromPoint at handle centers skips handles and returns the tile; 7 parent-child edges still render; console errors=0.

Real browser remeasure after handle fix: graph renders 8 two-row tiles, 1 focused node, 7 collapsed-count tabs, 7 parent-child edges, no console errors; handle-only change did not disturb the drill-down tree surface.
